sections.title: GENERAL PRINCIPLES
sections.text:
Representative commodities within each Classification 1 commodity group and subgroup will be selected and proposed, based on consideration of all available information. The following principles will be used for the selection of representative commodities: 
 A representative commodity is most likely to contain the highest residues. 
 A representative commodity is likely to be major in terms of production and/or consumption.  
 A representative commodity is most likely similar in morphology, growth habit, pest problems and edible portion to the related commodities within a group or subgroup. 
The application of the three principles in the selection of representative commodities is based on the assumption that all of the commodities, covered by the commodity group maximum residue limit ( MRL), are produced following a similar2 use pattern or good agricultural practice (GAP). 
To facilitate the global use of the commodity groups for MRLs, alternative representative commodities may be selected giving flexibility for use of residue research conducted in different countries or re gions that may vary due to regional differences in dietary consumption and/or areas of production for certain commodities.

sections.title: GUIDANCE AND PROCEDURES
sections.text: As proposals for the revision of the Classification are made and revised commodity groupings are developed and provided to CCPR for their review, proposals on representative commodities will also be provided in parallel with the respective commodity grouping  revisions and will advance through the Step Procedure for adoption by the Codex Alimentarius Commission (CAC). As comments are addressed on the revisions of the Classification and the proposed representative commodities and these are agreed by CCPR and adopted by CAC, two separate documents will be created and maintained: (1) the Classification (without mention of representative commodities) and (2) principles and guidance on the selection of representative commodities. The JMPR may be advised to use the representative commodities adopted by CAC. However, JMPR may use other representative commodities (including those which may be specifically requested by member nations)

sections.title: INTRODUCTION
sections.text: Residue extrapolation is the process by which the residue levels on representative commodities are utilised to estimate residue levels on related commodities in the same commodity group or subgroup for which trials have not been conducted. Representative commodities are chosen based on their commercial importance and the similarity of their morphology and residue characteristics to other related commodities in the group or subgroup. Ideally representative commodities are the most economically important commodities in production and/or consumption in a group or subgroup and have a greater dietary burden and have residue characteristics similar to other members of the group or subgroup. Residue extrapolation is  a common consideration utilised by regulators internationally for ensuring that data requirements are only at a level that is scientifically justified in conducting risk assessment and to ensure the regulatory process does not become unnecessarily burdensome especially for minor crops.  The objective of this document is to (1) propose criteria for the selection of representative commodities; (2) propose example representative commodities and (3) provide a detailed justification for the selection of the representative commodities.

sections.title: Note
sections.text: Table 1 in this document is provided to (1) separate the selection of representative commodities from the Classification itself; (2) propose examples of representative commodities in parallel with the respective Codex commodity grouping Classification revisions; (3) provide flexibility on the selection of representative crops and (4) provide guidance not only to the Committee on P esticide Residues (CCPR) and Codex members, but also to the Joint Meeting on Pesticide Residues ( JMPR), product manufacturers and other data generators. Detailed background information regarding production, consumption, MRLs and characteristics and justification for selection of the representative commodities according to the indicated principles were provided in working documents considered by the Committee when developing the representative commodities for each commodity group.

sections.title: ALTERNATIVE REPRESENTATIVE COMMODITIES
sections.text: To facilitate the global use of the commodity groups for MRLs, alternative representative commodities may be selected giving flexibility for use of residue research conducted in different countries or regions that may vary due to regional differences in dietary consumption and/or areas of production for certain commodities. Table 1 in this document proposes examples of representative commodities for commodity groups. Depending on country or regional differences, alternative representative commodities may be proposed by a country. For example, leeks may be proposed as an alternative representative commodity for green onions in the green onion subgroup of bulb vegetables.

sections.title: Selection of Representative Commodities
sections.text: When representative commodities are utilised to extrapolate residue levels to other members of a commodity group, it is on the assumption that residues in other members of the commodity group will not be significantly different to residues found in the representative commodity. That is, the representative commodities are good indicators of the upper range of residues likely to be encountered for the group or subgroup, based on the same or comparable GAP and other available information. An MRL for the group may be estimated from the highest residue level for any of the individual representative commodities or from the larger combined data set. The ALARA 3 principle should be considered in terms of whether the larger residue data set should be combined and the potential impact of derived values used in the dietary risk assessment.

Selection of Representative Commodities
level=1
When representative commodities are utilised to extrapolate residue levels to other members of a commodity group, it is on the assumption that residues in other members of the commodity group will not be significantly different to residues found in the representative commodity. That is, the representative commodities are good indicators of the upper range of residues likely to be encountered for the group or subgroup, based on the same or comparable GAP and other available information. An MRL for the group may be estimated from the highest residue level for any of the individual representative commodities or from the larger combined data set. The ALARA 3 principle should be considered in terms of whether the larger residue data set should be combined and the potential impact of derived values used in the dietary risk assessment.

An MRL for the group may be estimated from the highest residue level for any of the individual representative commodities or from the larger combined data set. The ALARA 3 principle should be considered in terms of whether the larger residue data set should be combined and the potential impact of derived values used in the dietary risk assessment.

sections.title: WIDER EXTRAPOLATIONS
sections.text: A representative commodity should meet at least the first two principles described above, i.e. likely to contain the highest residues and also major in terms of production and/or consumption. However, it may not always fit well with the growth habits, or pest problems of morphology within one group or subgroup. In such situations, extrapolations beyond the members of a commodity group may be appropriate. These can be considered on a case-by-case basis when commodities (with similar GAPs) have similar size, shape and surface area. Examples of these possible wider extrapolations include (1) translation of certain stone or pome fruit MRLs to a tropical fruit; (2) where residues are all <LOQ (limit of quantification) for pre-emergent herbicide uses and (3) seed treatments for non systemic pesticides.

sentences.original: Representative commodities within each Classification 1 commodity group and subgroup will be selected and proposed, based on consideration of all available information.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: To facilitate the global use of the commodity groups for MRLs, alternative representative commodities may be selected giving flexibility for use of residue research conducted in different countries or re gions that may vary due to regional differences in dietary consumption and/or areas of production for certain commodities.
classification.pattern: Purpose clause + modal verb phrase granting permission + participial phrase explaining flexibility + relative clauses detailing variability causes

components.purpose: To facilitate the global use of the commodity groups for MRLs
components.permission: alternative representative commodities may be selected
components.flexibility_reason: giving flexibility for use of residue research conducted in different countries or regions
components.variability_causes: regional differences in dietary consumption and/or areas of production for certain commodities

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission ('may be selected') with explanatory and conditional rationale spread across purpose and participial clauses, not matching any known rigid template structure; the modal verb and detailed contextual justification situate it firmly in Permission with a complex embedded rationale.
notes: The sentence complexly combines purpose, permission, and explanatory clauses, which makes strict template matching difficult.

reference.document: CXG 84-2012 - Principles and Guidance on the Selection of Representative Commodities for the Extrapolation of Maximum Residue Limits for Pesticides to Commodity Groups
reference.document_title: Principles and Guidance on the Selection of Representative Commodities for the Extrapolation of Maximum Residue Limits for Pesticides to Commodity Groups

sentences.original:
The following principles will be used for the selection of representative commodities: 
 A representative commodity is most likely to contain the highest residues. 
 A representative commodity is likely to be major in terms of production and/or consumption.  
 A representative commodity is most likely similar in morphology, growth habit, pest problems and edible portion to the related commodities within a group or subgroup. 
The application of the three principles in the selection of representative commodities is based on the assumption that all of the commodities, covered by the commodity group maximum residue limit ( MRL), are produced following a similar2 use pattern or good agricultural practice (GAP).

pages.title: PRINCIPLES AND GUIDANCE ON THE SELECTION OF REPRESENTATIVE COMMODITIES FOR THE EXTRAPOLATION OF MAXIMUM RESIDUE LIMITS FOR PESTICIDES TO COMMODITY GROUPS
pages.summary: A página contém um título principal em caixa alta, que funciona como nível 1, seguido de uma informação adicional sobre adoção e emenda que é associada a esse título. Não há seções ou subtítulos adicionais na página.

A página contém quatro títulos principais em nível 1: INTRODUCTION, GENERAL PRINCIPLES, Note e GUIDANCE AND PROCEDURES, todos com textos correspondentes bem relacionados. A hierarquia está coerente, sem níveis 2 ou inferiores, pois o texto está segmentado conforme os títulos principais. Não há indicações de conteúdo continuado entre páginas. A estrutura está adequada para suporte a RAG.

A página contém cinco títulos principais de nível 1 sem numeração: ALTERNATIVE REPRESENTATIVE COMMODITIES, PRECEDENCE IN SELECTION OF REPRESENTATIVE COMMODITIES, Selection of Representative Commodities, WIDER EXTRAPOLATIONS e um rodapé FOOTNOTE. O texto está associado aos títulos corretamente e indica que o conteúdo da primeira seção segue de página anterior e continua após esta página. A estrutura está adequada para suporte a RAG.

A página contém um título principal TABLE 1 e um subtítulo TYPE 01 - FRUIT COMMODITY GROUPS, que organizam diversos grupos e subgrupos de frutas. Identificam-se grupos e subgrupos numerados contendo listas explicativas. Todos os nós principais desta página aparecem visualmente. O conteúdo da última subcategoria Berries continua para a próxima página, bem como os níveis superiores. Não há título principal do documento identificado na página. A estrutura está adequada para suporte a RAG.

Página contém grupos e subgrupos de frutas estruturados hierarquicamente. Níveis 1 e 2 são identificados com número e título, texto associado detalhado por subgrupo. Grupos/subgrupos de frutas continuam de página anterior e/ou para próxima página. Cabeçalho identificado. Não há título principal do documento. Estrutura adequada para RAG.

Página contém subgrupos 005A e 005B claramente delimitados como títulos nível 1, com listagem detalhada de frutos em text_on_page. Subgrupo 005A continua da página anterior, 005B segue para próxima página. Cabeçalho identificado. Sem título principal do documento.

A página apresenta três principais seções: Subgroup 005C, Group 006, e dentro deste dois subgrupos 006A e 006B. Texto listado detalha as commodities de cada subgroup. Subgrupo 006B continua para próxima página. Sem título principal do documento, cabeçalho identificado.

Página contém quatro subgrupos principais (006C a 006F) com títulos numerados e descrições listando exemplos de commodities. O conteúdo da Subgroup 006C é continuação da página anterior, as demais começam nesta página. Não há título principal do documento visível. Cabeçalho identificado claramente. Estrutura hierárquica bem definida para uso em RAG.

Página contém uma tabela com estrutura hierárquica clara: TABLE 2, que inclui TYPE 02 VEGETABLE COMMODITY GROUPS, subdividido em Group 009, Group 010 e Group 11, com seus respectivos subgrupos. Cada nó tem texto associado e níveis definidos entre 1 e 2, sem continuidade explícita para página anterior, e um nó continua na próxima página (Subgroup 010C). O cabeçalho foi identificado e removido do texto interno para RAG.

A página contém grupos e subgrupos hierarquicamente organizados de vegetais frutíferos e folhosos. Foram identificados níveis 1 e 2, com agrupadores claros e texto explicativo associado a cada subtítulo. O cabeçalho repetitivo "CAC/GL 84-2012  10   Codex Group /  Subgroup" foi removido dos textos. Não há indicação explícita de continuidade para página anterior nem conteúdos que avancem para a próxima página.

A página contém seções principais Sobre Exemplos de Commodities Representativas e Subgrupo 013A, Leafy greens, com texto explicativo. A última seção indica continuidade para a próxima página. O cabeçalho é CAC/GL 84-2012  11   Codex Group /  Subgroup.

A página apresenta a seção principal "Examples of Representative Commodities" com diversos subgrupos classificados numericamente (Subgroup 013B a Subgroup 13 H). Cada subgrupo corresponde a um nível 2, contendo texto descritivo associado. A seção principal continua da página anterior e segue para a próxima página. Um dos subgrupos (Baby leaves) também indica que continua na próxima página.

A página contém a seção principal 'Examples of Representative Commodities' (nível 1) que continua da página anterior e segue para a próxima página. Contém subgrupos classificatórios de nível 2 e 3, com seus respectivos textos. Os títulos foram mapeados conforme suas numerações e nomes, e o texto associado está limpo sem cabeçalhos repetitivos.

A página contém a seção principal 'Examples of Representative Commodities' de nível 1, que continua da página anterior e segue para a próxima página. Dentro dela, há subgrupos de nível 2 e um sub-subgrupo de nível 3. O texto está associado a cada nó relevante, limpo do cabeçalho e organizado segundo a hierarquia de títulos detectada.

A página contém a seção principal 'EXAMPLES OF REPRESENTATIVE COMMODITIES' de nível 1, que continua da página anterior e segue para a próxima página. Dentro dela, há subgrupos de nível 2 e um subgrupo de nível 1 'Group 17' com seus respectivos subgrupos de nível 2. A página mostra texto associado a cada nó estrutural relevante, limpo de cabeçalhos e rodapés para uso em RAG.

Página apresenta a Tabela 3 como título principal sem numeração, contendo a seção tipo 03 de grupos de commodities de gramíneas como nível 1 sem numeração, incluindo o Grupo 020 Cereal Grains e seus subgrupos detalhados como níveis 2 e 3. Também contém o Grupo 021 de gramíneas para produção de açúcar. O conteúdo está limpo e estruturado para RAG, com indicação que a hierarquia é continuidade da página anterior e segue para a próxima.
